name: Asparagus Omelet
recipeIngredient: 1/4 tsp Crushed dried basil or 3/4 tsp Minced fresh basil
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut asparagus into 1-inch pieces.
2. Cook asparagus in boiling salted water until tender, about 2 to 4 minutes. Drain thoroughly.
3. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in an 8-inch skillet with a non-stick lining. Sauté garlic and mushrooms until done and moisture has evaporated. Remove from the pan; keep warm.
4. In a small bowl, combine eggs, milk, salt, basil (crushed dried or minced fresh), and pepper.
5. Melt remaining butter in the skillet until foamy, swirling it around to coat evenly.
6. When the pan is hot enough that a drop of water sizzles when dropped in, pour in the egg mixture.
7. Tip the pan so the eggs coat the skillet evenly. As the eggs cook, periodically lift up the cooked edges, tilt the pan, and let uncooked egg run underneath.
8. When the eggs are set but the surface is still shiny, place asparagus and mushrooms on one side of the omelet. Slide the omelet out of the pan, folding the side without vegetables over the top.
9. Serve immediately.
